_comment: Declarative Sentry alert rule definitions for exam-prep-master.
These rules are NOT applied automatically — Sentry's public API for
alert rules requires an org-level auth token that we do not keep in CI.
The intent of this file is twofold:
1. Be the source of truth for the alerts we expect in production,
so drift between code and Sentry UI is reviewable in git.
2. Provide ready-to-use payloads for `sentry-cli issues alert create`
After editing this file, apply changes in the Sentry UI:
Settings → Alerts → Create Alert → copy conditions from below.
See docs/monitoring.md for the step-by-step procedure.

issue_alerts.name: Error spike: new issue burst
issue_alerts.description: Fires when a new issue surfaces more than 10 times in 1 hour. Catches regressions immediately after a deploy.

issue_alerts.name: Error spike: sustained high volume
issue_alerts.description: Fires when any single issue fires 50+ times in 5 minutes — indicates a user-facing outage.

issue_alerts.name: Regression: previously resolved issue reopened
issue_alerts.description: Fires when an issue we marked as resolved starts erroring again. Usually means a bad deploy or a revert.

metric_alerts.name: Performance: frontend LCP degradation
metric_alerts.description: Largest Contentful Paint p75 exceeds 4s (Google 'poor' threshold) for 10 minutes. Signals a real UX regression, not a single slow session.

metric_alerts.name: Performance: page-load transaction p95 > 5s
metric_alerts.description: Catches a widespread slowdown on the initial page load — e.g. a bad bundle-size regression or a CDN problem.

metric_alerts.name: Reliability: frontend error rate > 2%
metric_alerts.description: Ratio of error events to total sessions exceeds 2% for 10 minutes. Tuned to ignore single-user noise while flagging real incidents.

metric_alerts.name: Reliability: crash-free session rate < 98%
metric_alerts.description: Fires if the rolling crash-free session rate falls below 98% for 15 minutes. Our SLO target is 99.5%.
